text.title: Project Integration Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the project-integration methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Project charter MUST be signed by Sponsor before delivery starts; un-signed charter = un-authorised project.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Cross-area decisions (e.g., scope-cut affecting schedule + cost) MUST be reflected in ALL impacted baselines.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Overall RAG MUST be roll-up of per-area RAG — never independent of areas.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: PM MUST have documented authority to escalate cross-area conflict; integrator without authority = decoration.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Baselines MUST carry version + approval date; replaced baselines retained in history.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for single-area projects or pre-charter discovery.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
